description_long: This object is a classic utility pole structure, typically found in urban and suburban environments for supporting overhead power or communication lines. It consists of two tall, cylindrical wooden poles set vertically into the ground, connected at the top by three parallel wires that are suspended between them. Each pole features a horizontal crossarm near the top, from which the wires are attached and tensioned, and additional support cables run from the crossarms to the poles for stability. The wood appears weathered and dark, with visible grain and some metallic hardware at the connection points. The wires are thin and metallic, stretching tautly between the poles, and there are no visible transformers, streetlights, or identification tags attached. The overall design is utilitarian, with a focus on function and durability for outdoor installation.
description: A pair of weathered wooden utility poles connected by three taut overhead wires, with horizontal crossarms and metallic hardware for support.
description_view_0: Two wooden poles with three parallel wires suspended between them, viewed at an angle showing both poles and the connecting wires.
description_view_1: A direct end-on view of one pole, showing the crossarm and wires extending away from the viewer.
description_view_2: Similar to view 0, showing both poles and wires from the opposite side, highlighting the symmetry of the structure.
description_view_3: A direct end-on view of the opposite pole, mirroring view 1 with the crossarm and wires extending away.
attribute_arrangement_of_crossarms: Each pole has a single horizontal crossarm mounted near the top, with both crossarms aligned at the same height.
